content.thinking:
The user is editing SlotReels.ts to import WANTED_POSTER_MANAGER_EVENT from WantedPosterManager. This is the second file modification in the implementation - first was adding the event handling to WantedPosterManager, now SlotReels is being prepared to dispatch that event.

This is part of implementing the synchronized exit of wanted posters with slot reel drop-out animation. The next step will likely be to actually dispatch the PLAY_OUT_ALL event from SlotReels.TriggerDropOut().

symbolNode.setPosition(targetPos);\\n                    resolve();\\n                })\\n                .start();\\n        });\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 開始落下所有Symbol\\n     * @param content.thinking:
This is the key implementation change! SlotReels.TriggerDropOut() now dispatches the PLAY_OUT_ALL event to WantedPosterManager right after the idempotency check passes. This completes the synchronization feature.

Let me observe what was changed:
1. Added event dispatch call to WANTED_POSTER_MANAGER_EVENT.PLAY_OUT_ALL
2. Placed after the idempotency return check (ensuring it only fires once per round)
3. Added detailed comments explaining:
   - This is triggered when old board drops out = new round start
   - Placed after idempotency check to ensure only fires once
   - Exit is fire-and-forget (async, doesn't block)
   - Later RebuildAll will skip PlayOut since items are already active=false

This completes the implementation of the feature requested in the original user message.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Completed Wanted Poster Synchronized Exit with Reel Drop-Out</title>
  <subtitle>SlotReels.TriggerDropOut now dispatches PLAY_OUT_ALL event to trigger poster exit animations synchronized with old board drop-out</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>eventManager.Dispatch(WANTED_POSTER_MANAGER_EVENT.PLAY_OUT_ALL) added to SlotReels.TriggerDropOut() method</fact>
    <fact>Event dispatch placed after idempotency check (m_dropAllSymbolsOutOfScreenPromise check) ensuring single dispatch per round</fact>
    <fact>Dispatch occurs at "spin start" timing when old board begins dropping out, before server ack arrives</fact>
    <fact>Fire-and-forget pattern allows poster exit animations to run in parallel with reel drop-out without blocking game flow</fact>
    <fact>Implementation leverages existing drop mode flow: cascade paths do not call TriggerDropOut so posters remain unaffected</fact>
    <fact>Comments document that RebuildAll will skip PlayOut loop for already-exited posters (active=false) and only execute PlayIn for new posters</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Completed the wanted poster synchronization feature by adding the critical event dispatch call in SlotReels.TriggerDropOut(). When a new spin begins and the old board starts its drop-out animation, SlotReels now immediately dispatches PLAY_OUT_ALL to WantedPosterManager, triggering all active posters to play their Out animations and deactivate in sync with the departing slot symbols. The dispatch is strategically placed after the idempotency check, ensuring it fires exactly once per round. The fire-and-forget pattern allows both animations (reel drop-out and poster exit) to run concurrently without blocking each other. When the server ack later arrives and SetNowRoundInfo calls RebuildAll, the posters are already inactive, so the PlayOut loop naturally becomes a no-op and only new PlayIn animations execute. This creates a seamless visual experience where wanted posters exit together with the old slot symbols, then new posters enter with the new symbols. The implementation is path-specific: only the spin start path (TriggerDropOut) triggers poster exit; cascade補牌 paths that don't call TriggerDropOut leave posters untouched, maintaining correct behavior across different game flows.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>assets/Script/SlotReels.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

content.thinking:
The user ran a full TypeScript compilation check and found 628 total errors, but looking at the output, all the errors shown are from:
1. Cocos Creator engine type definitions (cc.d.ts) - external library errors
2. Astarte framework type definitions (@types/editor.d.ts, electron.d.ts, extension.d.ts, message.d.ts) - framework type errors

None of these errors are related to the actual implementation of the wanted poster synchronization feature. These are pre-existing type errors in the project's dependencies and framework, not errors introduced by the new code changes.

The key point is that the previous check specifically filtered for "WantedPoster|SlotReels" and found zero errors, which confirms that the new implementation is type-safe.

This observation should note that the project has pre-existing type errors in external dependencies, but the wanted poster synchronization implementation itself introduces no new type errors.

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Project Has Pre-Existing Type Errors in External Dependencies</title>
  <subtitle>628 total TypeScript errors exist in Cocos Creator and Astarte framework definitions, but zero errors in implemented wanted poster feature</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Project contains 628 total TypeScript compilation errors from external dependencies</fact>
    <fact>All displayed errors originate from Cocos Creator engine type definitions (cc.d.ts) and Astarte framework types</fact>
    <fact>Error categories include: SystemEvent type conflicts, generic type argument mismatches, missing module declarations, duplicate index signatures</fact>
    <fact>Previous targeted grep for WantedPoster and SlotReels patterns returned zero errors confirming new implementation is clean</fact>
    <fact>Pre-existing errors do not affect runtime behavior as they are type definition mismatches in external libraries</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Full TypeScript compilation revealed that the project has 628 pre-existing type errors, but critically none of these are from the newly implemented wanted poster synchronization feature. All displayed errors originate from external dependencies: Cocos Creator 3.6.2 engine type definitions (cc.d.ts) containing generic type mismatches and missing module declarations, and Astarte framework type definitions with duplicate identifiers and index signature conflicts. These are known issues with third-party type definitions that do not impact runtime functionality. The earlier targeted check that filtered specifically for WantedPoster and SlotReels files confirmed zero errors in the actual implementation code. This means the wanted poster exit synchronization feature is fully type-safe and introduces no new type errors to the project. The pre-existing errors are environmental noise that should be addressed separately from feature development.</narrative>
